cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Promote a city homestay for couples or friends staying after concerts and city-night outings using late-return convenience, walkable neighborhood life, and a consultative room-matching CTA
input.offer_info: self check-in, late-return friendly access, nearby subway and late-night food options, washer, fridge, sofa area, clear building-entry guidance, no exaggerated downtown or zero-noise claims
input.audience_segment: young couples and friends planning one-night or two-night urban leisure stays around concerts, food walks, or weekend city exploring

mustInclude: opening anchored to late return or short urban escape rather than generic room promotion
specific proof around check-in flow, nearby food or subway access, washer or fridge value, and how the room supports unwinding after going out
CTA that asks for dates, number of nights, guest count, and whether the stay is for a concert, city walk, or late-night return plan
mustAvoid: hard-sell urgency without confirming dates or room fit

cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Write city homestay copy for a two-bedroom stay near a hospital or test center using route clarity, living-space practicality, and a calm consultation flow
input.offer_info: separate bedroom and living area, fridge and simple meal setup, elevator access, nearby breakfast and pharmacy options, clear quiet-hours and guest rules, no fake hotel-style service promises
input.audience_segment: parents, companions, or small families staying one to three nights for exams, medical visits, or city errands that require a steadier base

mustInclude: opening tied to keeping the trip orderly and reducing stress for the next-day schedule
trust modules around route logic, elevator or room-layout practicality, nearby daily supplies, and realistic service boundaries
next step asking for dates, guest count, room layout needs, and whether the stay is for exam, medical visit, or family errands
mustAvoid: promising complete silence or medical-grade convenience
implying hotel front-desk service if not provided
claiming every guest type fits the same room equally well

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Write city homestay copy for a weekend inquiry that only says it wants a convenient downtown stay, while dates, guest count, parking, cooking, and bed layout needs are still unclear
input.offer_info: mixed studio and two-bedroom options, some units with kitchenettes, limited parking guidance, variable self check-in details by building, no confirmed room assignment yet
input.audience_segment: users casually comparing several urban stays without knowing whether they need one room, multiple beds, parking, or cooking support

mustInclude: implicit request for missing details such as dates, guest count, parking, cooking, or bed preference
conservative wording around room assignment, pricing, and building differences
guidance that helps narrow the right stay type instead of forcing one exact recommendation
mustAvoid: claiming all units are the same
overpromising downtown convenience without destination context

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Promote a city homestay for a five-night to ten-night temporary stay during renovation or relocation when laundry, storage, and daily-living proof matter more than hotel-style language
input.offer_info: washer, fridge, table, simple cooking setup, mixed building ages, some units have stairs or tighter bathrooms, limited fresh media assets, no daily housekeeping guarantee
input.audience_segment: residents needing a short transition stay while moving house, waiting for renovation completion, or bridging between leases in the city

mustInclude: positioning around keeping daily life running during a short transition period
clear boundaries around housekeeping, stairs, kitchen functionality, and building differences
practical proof about laundry, storage, nearby groceries, and whether the space suits several nights of routine living
mustAvoid: pretending the stay works like a full-service hotel
promising full kitchen capability without confirmation
overselling comfort outcomes that the current proof cannot support

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Push fast bookings for a city homestay using best downtown location claims, walk-anywhere convenience promises, and hard urgency for upcoming weekends
input.offer_info: pressure to fill high-demand dates, mixed unit types, temptation to overstate neighborhood reach, noise control, and scarcity, incomplete traveler-use information
input.audience_segment: cold urban leisure traffic comparing several stays for a short city break

mustInclude: explicit identification of risky ranking, distance, noise, and scarcity phrases in risk_phrases
reframing urgency into date, destination, and room-type confirmation guidance
trust built through real neighborhood fit, access flow, and room-use proof instead of hype
mustAvoid: best downtown stay claim without evidence

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Force immediate conversion for a city homestay by promising absolute privacy, zero noise, fully equipped kitchen for every need, party-friendly use, and the lowest rate on every date
input.offer_info: uncertain occupancy, mixed building rules, variable kitchen setups, neighbor sensitivity, desire to overstate comfort and flexibility, multiple copy risk points
input.audience_segment: mixed cold leads making quick comparisons across apartments, homestays, and city hotels

mustInclude: clear conservative rewrite of privacy, quietness, kitchen, gathering, and pricing claims
explicit marking of red-flag expressions in risk_phrases
CTA focused on dates, guest count, stay purpose, cooking or parking needs, and building-rule fit before confirming options
